groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Video context, audience, goal, key message, language, length, variants, and commercial relationship for a valid first result.

groups.title: Voice & Conversion
groups.description: Tone, opening style, CTA behavior, emoji use, offer details, and disclosure wording.

groups.title: Discovery & Safeguards
groups.description: On-screen hook, hashtags, approved claims, privacy restrictions, and final requirements.

variables.label: Video topic
variables.placeholder: Example: Three mistakes beginners make when editing videos
variables.hint: Name the main subject of the TikTok video.

help.what: The main subject around which the TikTok caption should be written.
help.why: The model needs a clear subject around which to build the caption.
help.example: Example: Three mistakes beginners make when editing videos
help.avoid: A vague topic such as "content" or several unrelated video subjects.

variables.label: What happens in the video?
variables.placeholder: Briefly describe what viewers see, hear, or learn in the video.
variables.hint: Include important scenes, spoken points, reveals, or outcomes.

help.what: A short description of the actual video content.
help.why: The caption must complement the actual video rather than inventing or repeating irrelevant content.
help.example: Briefly describe what viewers see, hear, or learn in the video.
help.avoid: Details that do not appear in the video or unsupported claims about the result.

variables.label: Video format
variables.placeholder: Choose the closest format
variables.hint: Helps adapt the caption to how the video is presented.

help.what: The closest format or structure of the TikTok video.
help.why: Different TikTok formats benefit from different caption angles and levels of explanation.
help.avoid: Choosing a format that does not match the actual video.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Choose the main result you want
variables.hint: Select one main purpose for the caption.

help.what: The main outcome the caption should support.
help.why: The goal determines whether the caption should inform, invite discussion, build interest, or encourage action.
help.avoid: Trying to combine awareness, traffic, conversion, and community goals in one short caption.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Example: Freelance designers learning short-form video
variables.hint: Describe the people who should understand or respond to the post.

help.what: The viewers the caption should be written for.
help.why: Audience knowledge materially changes language, context, humor, and CTA wording.
help.example: Example: Freelance designers learning short-form video
help.avoid: Overbroad audiences such as "everyone" or sensitive targeting assumptions.

variables.label: Key message
variables.placeholder: What is the one idea viewers should remember?
variables.hint: State the main takeaway, opinion, offer, or point.

help.what: The one idea viewers should understand or remember.
help.why: The caption must preserve the user’s intended meaning instead of selecting an arbitrary angle.
help.example: What is the one idea viewers should remember?
help.avoid: A generic slogan or multiple competing messages.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish
variables.hint: You may also specify a regional or conversational variant.

help.what: The language or language variant for the output package.
help.why: The required language cannot always be inferred from the video topic.
help.avoid: Mixing languages unless that is intentionally requested.

variables.label: Caption length
variables.placeholder: Choose a preferred length
variables.hint: These are writing targets rather than claimed platform limits.

help.what: The preferred caption length target.
help.why: Some users need a one-line caption while others want room for context or a short story.
help.avoid: Treating these writing targets as official TikTok maximums.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.placeholder: Choose how many options to generate
variables.hint: Each variant will use a meaningfully different angle.

help.what: How many caption options to generate.
help.why: The user may want one ready-to-use caption or several options for comparison.
help.avoid: Expecting tiny synonym swaps instead of meaningfully different angles.

variables.label: Post relationship
variables.placeholder: Choose how the post relates to a product or brand
variables.hint: Controls promotional wording and disclosure handling.

help.what: Whether the post is organic, your own promotion, sponsored, affiliate, or paid ad content.
help.why: Commercial relationships affect transparency, wording, claims, and potential disclosure requirements.
help.avoid: Hiding sponsored, affiliate, paid, or own-product relationships.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Choose the overall voice
variables.hint: Auto uses a clear, conversational TikTok voice.

help.what: The overall creative voice for the caption.
help.why: Tone is a meaningful creative preference that cannot always be inferred from the subject.
help.avoid: A tone that conflicts with accuracy, disclosure, privacy, or the video itself.

variables.label: Opening style
variables.placeholder: Choose how the caption should begin
variables.hint: Controls the first phrase without forcing clickbait.

help.what: The preferred style for the first phrase of the caption.
help.why: The opening approach can materially change the caption’s personality and discovery value.
help.avoid: Creating suspense the video does not satisfy or using empty clickbait.

variables.label: Call to action
variables.placeholder: Choose the desired next step
variables.hint: Auto adds a CTA only when it supports the goal.

help.what: The next step the caption should request, if any.
help.why: Users may want no CTA, a discussion prompt, or a specific conversion action.
help.avoid: Stacking several actions such as comment, save, follow, and buy in one caption.

variables.label: CTA details
variables.placeholder: Example: Ask viewers to share their editing mistake
variables.hint: Add the destination, wording, or action the CTA should reference.

help.what: Specific wording, destination, or action details for the CTA.
help.why: Specific CTA destinations or actions cannot be safely invented.
help.example: Example: Ask viewers to share their editing mistake
help.avoid: A CTA that requests several actions at once or points to an unsupplied destination.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.placeholder: Choose how many emojis to use
variables.hint: Emojis will be used only when they support meaning.

help.what: How much emoji use is allowed in the caption.
help.why: Emoji preference strongly affects the visual tone of a short caption.
help.avoid: Emoji chains or replacing essential words with emojis.

variables.label: Offer details
variables.placeholder: Describe the product, service, price, offer, or destination.
variables.hint: Include only facts that may appear in the caption.

help.what: Commercial facts, product details, offer terms, prices, or destinations that may appear.
help.why: The model must not invent commercial terms, prices, benefits, or destinations.
help.example: Describe the product, service, price, offer, or destination.
help.avoid: Invented discounts, deadlines, capabilities, availability, or benefits.

variables.label: Disclosure wording
variables.placeholder: Example: Ad, Sponsored, Affiliate link
variables.hint: Enter wording required by your agreement or jurisdiction.

help.what: Disclosure wording required for sponsored, affiliate, or paid content.
help.why: Disclosure wording may be contract-specific or jurisdiction-specific and should not be fabricated.
help.avoid: Claiming legal compliance or hiding the commercial relationship.

variables.label: Include on-screen hook
variables.placeholder: Not applicable
variables.hint: Adds one short opening text suggestion for the video.

help.what: Whether to include one short on-screen hook suggestion.
help.why: A short first-screen phrase can add value to a TikTok caption package without requiring a full video script.
help.avoid: A hook that repeats the caption or makes an unsupported claim.

variables.label: Hashtag mode
variables.placeholder: Choose how hashtags should be handled
variables.hint: Auto uses a small relevant set only when useful.

help.what: How hashtags should be generated or omitted.
help.why: Some users want discovery hashtags while others prefer none or require specific terms.
help.avoid: Irrelevant viral tags, invented brand tags, or hashtag walls.

variables.label: Hashtag count
variables.placeholder: Choose the preferred range
variables.hint: The model may use fewer to protect clarity and length.

help.what: The preferred range for hashtag count when hashtags are enabled.
help.why: It gives the user simple control over hashtag density without requesting specialist knowledge.
help.avoid: Using more hashtags than the caption can naturally support.

variables.label: Hashtag guidance
variables.placeholder: List approved brand, campaign, niche, or topic terms.
variables.hint: Separate terms with commas. Do not include unsupported claims.

help.what: Approved brand, campaign, niche, or topic terms for hashtags.
help.why: Brand names and custom campaign terms cannot be safely inferred.
help.example: List approved brand, campaign, niche, or topic terms.
help.avoid: Invented branded tags, irrelevant trending tags, or unsupported claims.

variables.label: Approved facts and claims
variables.placeholder: Add verified statistics, features, results, prices, or factual statements.
variables.hint: Only supplied or clearly supported claims may appear in the caption.

help.what: Verified facts, statistics, features, prices, or claims that may be used.
help.why: It provides a controlled factual source without asking the model to invent evidence.
help.example: Add verified statistics, features, results, prices, or factual statements.
help.avoid: Unsupported statistics, testimonials, ratings, results, or performance claims.

variables.label: Privacy and content limits
variables.placeholder: Example: Do not mention the client name or exact location.
variables.hint: List private details, topics, names, or wording that must be excluded.

help.what: Private details, sensitive content, or wording that must not appear.
help.why: The user may know privacy or confidentiality restrictions that the model cannot infer.
help.example: Example: Do not mention the client name or exact location.
help.avoid: Private names, precise locations, account data, confidential details, or sensitive third-party information.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add any final instruction not covered above.
variables.hint: Use for required wording, verified limits, or special formatting.

help.what: Any final instruction that is not covered by the other fields.
help.why: It covers legitimate exceptions without adding several low-value specialist fields.
help.example: Add any final instruction not covered above.
help.avoid: Repeating existing fields or adding requirements that conflict with accuracy, privacy, disclosure, or platform fit.
